title: [ipv6_addr.h:64-87] in6_addr fallback detection cascade has false positive risk
description:
Gap found: ipv6_addr.h struct detection not reviewed.

Why this was missed: Header preprocessing complexity not analyzed.

The actual bug: The detection cascade (lines 64-87) tries to determine if struct in6_addr is already defined. The checks include _SYS_SOCKET_H (line 72) which indicates socket.h was included, but socket.h doesn't necessarily define in6_addr - that's in netinet/in.h. If socket.h is included without netinet/in.h, the fallback is skipped but in6_addr is undefined, causing compile errors. Similarly, _STRUCT_IN6_ADDR on macOS (line 72) is specific to the internal implementation and may change. The fallback mechanism could be simplified: just check for IN6ADDR_ANY_INIT which is reliably defined alongside struct in6_addr on all POSIX systems.
close_reason: Duplicate of tvfm.109: The _SYS_SOCKET_H false positive described in this issue was already fixed by tvfm.109. The current code no longer checks _SYS_SOCKET_H, and documentation at lines 56-59 explains why it was removed.
